sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the maze structure more clearly:
- Player is at rows 33-36, cols ~31-35 (the YOOOOY area)
- The player is surrounded by walls (B) on left and right
- Above the player (rows 31-32): SSSSSSSSSBBBBBBBBSSSSSSSSSSSSSSS - walls on left, open on right
- Below the player (rows 37-38): similar pattern
- To the left of the player: BBBBBBBBBB (walls)
- To the right of the player: BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB (walls)
Wait, but looking at the image, there's clearly a path going RIGHT from the player. Let me look at the full row 34:
Row 34: SSSSSSSSSSSSSSBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBYOOOOYBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBSSSSSS
So from left to right:
- SSSSSSSSSSSSSS (cols 0-13): open
- BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB (cols 14-29): walls
- YOOOOY (cols 30-35): player
- BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB (cols 36-51): walls
- SSSSSS (cols 52-57): open
The player is trapped between walls on both sides. But looking at the image, there's a corridor going right. Let me look at the rows above and below more carefully.
Row 32: SSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSBYYYYYYBbSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSBBSSSSSS
Row 33: SSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSBYOOOOYBbSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSBBSSSSSS
Row 34: SSSSSSSSSSSSSSBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBYOOOOYBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBSSSSSS
Row 35: SSSSSSSSSSSSSSBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBYOOOOYBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBSSSSSS
Row 36: SSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSBYOOOOYBbSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSBBSSSSSS
Row 37: SSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSBYYYYYYBbSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSBBSSSSSS
So the player is in a 4x4 cell (rows 33-36, cols 31-35). The walls are:
- Left: cols 14-30 are B (wall)
- Right: cols 36-51 are B (wall)
- Above: row 32 has B at cols 29-30, then YYYYYY at cols 31-36, then B at col 37
- Below: row 37 has similar pattern
Wait, the player occupies rows 33-36. Looking at row 32 (above player):
- B at col 29-30, Y at cols 31-36, b at col 37
- The 'b' at col 37 is a thin wall piece
And row 36 (below player):
- B at col 29-30, Y at cols 31-36, b at col 37
So the player cell has:
- Top: Y border (row 32, cols 31-36)
- Bottom: Y border (row 37, cols 31-36)
- Left: B wall (cols 14-30)
- Right: B wall (cols 36-51)
But there's a 'b' (dark blue) at col 37, which is thinner than the B walls. This might be a passage!
Looking at the image more carefully, I see:
- The player is in a cell with a corridor going to the RIGHT
- The corridor goes right, then up to the top grid
- There's also a corridor going LEFT, then DOWN to the bottom grid
But from the ascii, the walls seem to block movement. Maybe the player moves by clicking (MOUSE) rather than arrow keys? Or maybe the walls are passable?
Wait, let me re-read the valid actions: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7. The player can move with arrow keys.
Let me try moving RIGHT from the player position to see what happens. The player center is at (34, 32). Let me try RIGHT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try moving RIGHT from the player to see if there's a passage
action([{'action': 'RIGHT'}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
